Some Response implementations¶

Immediate and blocking¶

class services.responses.ImmediateAndBlocking(Response)¶

Implements an immediate call to the service handler, the handler is executed in the main thread of the program. This is blocking in the sense that while the service is executing, the server cannot accept requests. This can be used when service handlers carry out some very simple and quick tasks.

Immediate and non blocking¶

class services.responses.ImmediateAndNonBlocking(Response)¶

This is the default mode used by the Services.Server.Server.

Implements an immediate call to the service handler, however the handler is executed in a different thread of the program. This is not blocking, because the server can accept new requests in the main thread of the program while the service handler executes in a different one. This can be used to optimise server availability and when service handlers do not take too long. While the service handler is running, the client is blocked with an open connection, awaiting a response.

More will need to be implemented, e.g. Queued and non blocking and queued and call back.
